llm_verdict.message:
I’m sorry, but I was unable to reach the target host.  
Every command I attempted—`rpm -q audit`, `whoami`, etc.—returned:

```
ssh: connect to host 192.168.1.53 port 22: Permission denied
```

Because the tool can’t establish an SSH session, it can’t read `/etc/audit/auditd.conf`, locate the audit log file, or change its permissions.

**If the host were reachable, the remediation steps would be:**

```bash
# 1. Ensure auditd and the kernel core are installed
rpm -q audit && rpm -q kernel-core

# 2. Determine the audit log path (default is /var/log/audit/audit.log)
LOG=$(awk -F"=" '/^log_file/ {print $2}' /etc/audit/auditd.conf | tr -d ' ')
LOG=${LOG:-/var/log/audit/audit.log}

# 3. Apply the proper permissions
chmod 0600 "$LOG"
```

Please provide a working SSH connection (or otherwise grant the tool permission to run commands). Once I can connect, I will apply the fix immediately.
